title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: March 10, 2026: Market Sentiment Rebounds on Easing Geopolitical Tensions

items.headline: Global Markets Rally as Oil Prices Retreat on Hopes for Iran Conflict De-escalation
items.summary: Global shares surged on Tuesday, echoing a rally on Wall Street, as investor sentiment improved significantly. The positive shift comes amidst signals from President Trump indicating that the war with Iran may conclude 'very soon,' leading to a sharp decline in crude oil prices. This has assuaged fears of escalating energy costs and geopolitical instability, despite earlier volatility.

items.headline: Shriram Finance Leads Gains
items.summary: Indian financial services firm Shriram Finance surged, benefiting from renewed investor confidence.

items.headline: Eicher Motors Climbs on Strong Sentiment
items.summary: Automotive manufacturer Eicher Motors saw significant gains as risk sentiment improved across the market.

items.headline: Tata Motors PV Rises
items.summary: Tata Motors' passenger vehicle unit experienced an uptick, aligning with the broader market rebound.

items.headline: Infosys Declines Amid Sector Underperformance
items.summary: Indian IT giant Infosys closed lower as the technology sector faced pressure.

items.headline: Eternal (Zomato) Faces Losses
items.summary: Food delivery platform Eternal (Zomato) was among the top losers for the day.

items.headline: Indian Sectors See Rotation; Auto Leads, IT Lags
items.summary: In the Indian market, the Nifty Auto index emerged as the top performer, surging 3.10%, with Consumer Durables also showing strong gains at 2.67%. Fertilizer stocks rallied significantly due to new government policies and hopes of de-escalation in the US-Iran conflict. Conversely, the Nifty IT and Nifty Oil and Gas indices were among the worst performers.

items.headline: Iran Conflict's Economic Impact Monitored Amidst De-escalation Hopes
items.summary: While markets reacted positively to de-escalation signals, the war with Iran continues to have a collateral impact on the world economy, driving up energy and fertilizer prices and threatening food shortages. The International Monetary Fund warns that every 10% increase in oil prices could push global inflation up by 0.4 percentage points. G7 energy ministers are scheduled to discuss releasing oil reserves.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Poised to Hold Rates, Treasury Yields Decline
items.summary: The CME Group's FedWatch tool indicates a 97.4% likelihood of the Federal Reserve maintaining current interest rates in March. Concurrently, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield saw a decrease, floating near 4.10%.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports: Kohl's Surpasses, Oracle Anticipated, Vail Resorts Down
items.summary: Kohl's ($KSS) surpassed its Q4 earnings estimates with a 25.29% surprise. Oracle Corp. ($ORCL) is expected to report its earnings after the closing bell. Meanwhile, Vail Resorts Inc. ($MTN) saw a 1.28% decline after reporting downbeat Q2 earnings and reduced fiscal 2026 guidance. Apyx Medical ($APYX) also had its Q4 2025 earnings call today.
